question_text:
K-th Smallest in Lexicographical Order

Given two integers n and k, return the kth lexicographically smallest integer in the range [1, n].

Constraints:

  • 1 <= k <= n <= 109
output_format: The kth lexicographically smallest integer in [1, n].
explanation:
10-ary Tree Traversal

Think of integers 1..n as a 10-ary prefix trie. Walk the trie: at each node, count how many numbers are under the subtree rooted at prefix cur to next=cur+1. If count < k, skip this subtree (cur++, k-=count). Otherwise, go deeper (cur*=10, k-=1). Count is computed as min(n+1, next) - cur for each level of the subtree.

Time: O(log²N). Space: O(1).
